# ADR-0008: The registry is regenerated by scan-on-demand, not event-driven (the live-symlink seam)

- **Status:** Accepted (design-stage)
- **Date:** 2026-06-05
- **Phase:** v0.2

## Context

A registry of artifact identity could be maintained two ways: **event-driven** (a hook/watcher updates a
record the instant a file changes) or **scan-on-demand** (the registry is *generated* by walking the
library when asked, and compared to the committed copy).

The decisive fact is forge's install mechanism. `forge install` registers this repo as a Claude Code
plugin and **symlinks the marketplace `installLocation` straight at the repo** (`bin/forge.mjs` install
docs: "a SYMLINK … so edits to the library are live (no copy to keep in sync)"). Because the install is a
live symlink, **editing `agents/code-reviewer.md` changes the live artifact with no `forge` command, no
hook, and no install event** to hang an update on. There is simply no reliable choke point where an
event-driven registry could observe the change. A file watcher would be a daemon — and foundational
invariant 4 (fail-open) plus `ADR-0010` (no daemon) forbid one.

## Decision

**The registry is GENERATED by scanning the library on demand, and is never hand-edited.**

- `forge registry build [--write]` walks the library dirs — `agents/ skills/ commands/ rules/ hooks/
  bundles/`, plus validators in `lint/`, meta-tests in `tests/meta/`, and engine scripts under
  `bootstrap/` — resolving each to a `uid` with the **same kind→path resolution as
  `validate-manifests.mjs`** (extracted into shared `forge/manager/lib/resolve-kind.mjs` +
  `lib/frontmatter.mjs`). Hooks resolve to ids in `hooks/hooks.json`, not to files.
- `build` (no `--write`) rebuilds **in memory** and is read-only (dry-run by default, C4). `--write`
  persists to `forge/.forge/registry.json` and appends mutation lines to `registry.log.jsonl`.
- A new auto-discovered `lint/validate-registry.mjs` (`ADR-0014`) rebuilds in memory and **compares to the
  committed file**; a mismatch is an **ERROR**: `"registry stale, run forge registry build --write"`.
- Because no event can be hung on a content change, the "hash changed but `revision` not incremented" check
  is **scan-based and advisory** — a `WARN`, never a commit block (`ADR-0007`). The scan, not an event, is
  the only place drift can be noticed, and a solo dev must not be blocked by it.

## Consequences

**Positive**
- Correct under the live symlink: the registry reflects reality at scan time, regardless of how the change
  arrived (editor, `git checkout`, branch switch).
- Deterministic and reproducible: rebuild-from-scratch always yields the same registry for the same tree,
  so "is the committed registry stale?" is a clean equality check.
- No daemon, no watcher, zero new always-on cost — honors fail-open and no-daemon.

**Negative**
- The registry can be *stale between scans*; nothing updates it live. Mitigated: `validate-registry` runs
  in the existing validator sweep (`lint/run-all.mjs`) and CI, so staleness surfaces on every check.
- A scan walks the tree each time. Cheap at ~93 files; acceptable.

**Neutral**
- This is why the bump gate (`ADR-0007`) is advisory and why versioning is fold-based (`ADR-0006`): all
  three follow from there being no event seam.

## Alternatives considered

- **Filesystem watcher / daemon** — rejected: violates no-daemon (`ADR-0010`) and fail-open; a crashed
  watcher would silently desync the registry.
- **Update on `forge install`/`sync` only** — rejected: the live symlink means most edits never go through
  those commands, so the registry would miss the majority of changes.
- **Git pre-commit hook as the event** — rejected: edits in the working tree before commit (and the
  `--no-verify` escape that forge's own `block-no-verify` hook fights) make this both late and bypassable.

## Related

ADR-0005, ADR-0006, ADR-0007 (advisory gate this enables), ADR-0010 (no daemon), ADR-0014 (the
self-validator), C5, BR-REG, BR-VER, SPEC-01, SPEC-02, SPEC-03.
